report_metadata.report_title: Exposing RDStealer — Deep Dive Into A Targeted Cyber-attack Against East-asia Infrastructure

ip_addresses.evidence_snippet: The C2 information obtained from the config strings of identified samples gives us insight into the attackers’ preferences for choosing the infrastructure provider:

Besides the C2 addresses from the Logutil configs, one more domain is used by attackers, which was obtained from two AsyncRat samples – one standalone executable located at %PROGRAM_FILES% -secure\psb\diagnostics s_ui.exe

domains.evidence_snippet: The C2 information obtained from the config strings of identified samples gives us insight into the attackers’ preferences for choosing the infrastructure provider:

The C2 addresses from the Logutil configs, one more domain is used by attackers, which was obtained from two AsyncRat samples – one standalone executable located at %PROGRAM_FILES% -secure\psb\diagnostics s_ui.exe

tools.name: Logutil backdoor
tools.evidence_snippet: The Logutil backdoor implements the usual capabilities that are needed to maintain the foothold in the victim’s network - capabilities such as file download/upload and command execution.

tools.name: AsyncRat
tools.evidence_snippet: AsyncRat was also used at the earlier stages of infection.

summary: This report exposes a targeted cyber-attack against East-asia infrastructure, where attackers used the Logutil backdoor to maintain foothold in the victim’s network and collect sensitive information.

timeline.evidence_snippet: Earliest evidence of exploitation of CVE-2023-34362 occurred on May 27, 2023, when threat actors began targeting MOVEit Transfer installations.
